candidate_summary: Maps verge-and-foliot 'frictional-rest' vs pendulum 'detached' escapement to LR controller design: a detached regulator with internal state on slower clock, releasing setpoints periodically, yields wider basin-stability under gradient noise than a frictional/directly-coupled LR controller.
hit_rule: hit = (kw_count>=2) OR (cos>=0.7) OR (judge>=0.7); kw_count counts case-insensitive substring presence of content_words in title+snippet.

results.title: Augmented-GDA: LLM In-Loop Gradient Descent Algorithm (FLLM 2025)
results.judge_reason: Augmented-GDA separates static/dynamic channels for LR-like decisions and reduces tuning dispersion — superficially related to dual-channel control but does not invoke escapement-style detached oscillator dynamics or basin-stability under gradient noise.

results.title: Gradient Descent with Provably Tuned Learning-rate Schedules
results.judge_reason: Provides analytical tuning tools for LR schedules but does not separate the regulator from instantaneous gradient feedback or claim basin-stability advantages from decoupling.

results.title: Optimal Learning Rate Schedule for Balancing Effort and Performance
results.judge_reason: An optimal LR schedule analysis trading effort vs performance; no detached-regulator framing, no escapement analogy, no basin-stability claim under gradient noise.

results.title: Why Linearly Decaying the LR to Zero (OpenReview)
results.judge_reason: Argues vanishing LR works when gradient noise is the bottleneck — touches the gradient-noise concern but does not propose a detached regulator-state on a slower clock.

results.title: Friction and Dynamics of Verge and Foliot (MDPI)
results.judge_reason: Source-domain physics of verge-foliot friction dynamics; confirms the mechanism but is not an LR/training application, so functional equivalence to the LLM claim is low.

results.title: Verge escapement (Wikipedia)
results.judge_reason: Encyclopedic source-side reference describing foliot inertia and lack of detachment from drive train; no LR/training-loop content.

results.title: Dynamics of the verge and foliot clock escapement (arXiv 2016, pre-2024)
results.judge_reason: Pre-2024 mechanical-dynamics paper on verge-foliot; source-side only, no training/LR transfer.

results.title: Learning rate (Wikipedia)
results.judge_reason: General LR encyclopedia entry covering step size, schedules, decay, adaptive methods — no detached-regulator or escapement-style decoupling framing.

verdict: Candidate is NOT covered as a unified analogy. Source-side (verge/foliot mechanics) is well-covered by hits 4,5,6, and LLM-side LR-schedule prior art is present (results 0,1,2,3,7), but no single result frames LR control as a detached-vs-frictional-rest oscillator with periodic escapement releases and basin-stability claim. The specific cross-domain mapping appears novel within this corpus.
